children.value: An interactive, modern periodic table web application featuring 3D visualizations and detailed element information. Built with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
Click any element to view detailed information
Color-coded by element category (alkali metals, halogens, noble gases, etc.)
Responsive grid layout that adapts to different screen sizes
Interactive 3D models of each element’s Bohr model
Atomic mass, period, group, and phase

html2/
├── index.html              # Main HTML file
├── styles.css              # Complete styling
├── script.js               # Application logic
├── periodic-data.js        # Periodic table data (auto-generated)
├── server.py               # Local development server
└── Periodic-Table-JSON-master/
    └── PeriodicTableJSON.json  # Source data

# Navigate to the html2 directory
cd html2

# Run the server
python3 server.py
and automatically open in your browser.

# Install a simple HTTP server
npm install -g http-server

# Run it
http-server .
directly in your browser (Note: Some features may not work due to CORS restrictions)
: The periodic table displays all 118 elements in their correct positions
: Click any element to open the detailed modal
: Rotate, zoom, and interact with the 3D Bohr model
: Use the tabs to view different visualizations:
: Click the X button, click outside, or press Escape
Two-finger pinch on mobile to zoom
View in AR (if supported by your device)
: Atomic properties, physical properties, images, 3D models, spectral data
Desktop (1200px+): Full layout with side-by-side visualization and info
Mobile (480px-767px): Compact periodic table with optimized spacing
Note: The 3D model viewer requires WebGL support.
Some 3D models may not load if external CDN is unavailable
AR functionality requires a compatible device and browser
Spectral images may not be available for all elements
Some historical data may be null for undiscovered or synthetic elements
This project uses the periodic table data under its original license. The interface and code are provided as-is for educational and personal use.
Ensure all files are in the correct directory

prev.title: Quick Start Guide - Interactive Periodic Table
